transcript.clean_text: stage four of la welter a sprinting day we weren't too sure would the wind blow up would the ramps at the end compromise some of the pure sprinters and allow the likes of matthews or trenton to have some fun on the approach to the line well a look at the profile a short day perhaps with a long transfer top and tail of it no climbing a rolling terrain however with an intermediate sprint to tempt those in the breakaway canal and modrato made it for burgos in their plum coloured buu though it was that was going for the intermediate sprint and the sprint far enough away from the finish to also offer up some bonus points for those battling for the green jersey sitting on the back of jasper philipson well the breakaway went through it was boo from oscar tell that took the points the majority of them still 13 and 10 up for grabs by those following on seneschal took some points to deny jacobson's rivals but phillips and for albertson fenix coming home for 10 solidifying his place in green well yes we're phillips and favorites for the end of the day doesn't mind a rap neither does arno de mar and as we approach town the pace was picking up they were worried about cross winds coming out from behind the lee of a hill but in the end the wind didn't blow the storm was to be picked up by the fast man at the end and no skirmish offered up by the breakaway they shook hands accepting their fate and with 13 and a half kilometers to go it was all settling in into colour order by the time we were approaching the line the technical finale was causing a bit of a compression and i'm afraid that our leader went down ryan taramay on the deck but within the three kilometer rule and as a result stays in red as we will ride on tomorrow with two more sprinting stages this still had to be dealt with and demar was bossing it group armor francis you're in a fantastic position madolo went off early expecting jasper phillipson's green jersey to be on his back wheel he wasn't there he'd been boxed out of it a few others had as well demar looked likely but behind him was screened fabio jacobson who came through to take it out the line superb win for jacobson and in these conditions that will bode very well for him going forward it's been a long road back he's got there the white hot nature of sprinting now offering up no fear or trepidation for fabio jacobson there were some big men out there that he beat some top sprinters and he king of the hill he's also in the green jersey and the confidence just continues to rise for the dutchman magnus courtney hillson finishing in third place just behind demar and of dnasey and michael matthews aligarh maos trenton phillipson and minali phillipson not very happy about being boxed in he's got another chance to make amends in the next two days of sprinting coming home bruised unbowed ryan tara may stays in the red jersey going forward tara may still leading by the same margin that three kilometer rule saving his leadership for now so two more sprinting days still to come on le welter next up the run to alba seti stage five 184 kilometers and more will the wind play a part well we hope so because there's no climbing and it's a stage that's even flatter than stage four should be a dramatic sprint finish almost guaranteed we look forward to it we'll see you then
